threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the Underground' instead of just 'the Tube.' It’s like a first date mistake here.
Heard a couple arguing on the 35 last night; she was convinced the bus was taking them 'the long way round' to Clapham, while he was just trying to read his paper in peace.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was in Neukölln when I saw a guy juggling at Hermannplatz while a cat sat next to him, completely unfazed by the chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you see whole streets turn into open-air galleries during gallery weekends—artists painting live while you sip on coffee from a stand.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to carry an extra pair of shoes if they're taking BART after 5. Nothing ruins a Friday evening like overheated brakes and a forgotten backup plan.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a suit serenading the bus stop with an old Motown tune. The guitar? A broken broomstick. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they’re avoiding the crowds by coming to Gràcia, but they end up clumping in the same plazas like it’s a tourist trap. It’s wild.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their bewildered look when the tram suddenly stops, waiting for the traffic light to change – it’s like a rite of passage here.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You know you're new when you still look puzzled at the 'no cycling' signs near the park while everyone else seems to know the rules by heart.
Caught a night bus from Brixton to Croydon last weekend, and a guy was trying to barter a pint of lager for a phone charger. The driver didn't bat an eye.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: It hit me when the bakery lady started remembering my regular order, and the kids in the playground waved while I walked by. That's home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you catch a graffiti artist working live on the U-Bahn just moments before a train rolls in. It's like art meets transit chaos.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they can name the exact month the taqueria on 24th changed their salsa recipe—and the year they went cashless.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the bus this morning. Turns out he was headed to a wedding. Only in the Tenderloin do you get black tie in the daytime.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think La Rambla is the heart of the city, but they miss the real vibe in Gràcia’s plazas, where locals gather and life feels genuine.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always spot a recent arrival by their bewilderment staring at the T-10 ticket machines, utterly baffled by the options.
